Has this innovation, or planned change, been shown to make a positive difference to care and/or outcomes?
How certain is the research evidence?
crumbs: 1  Context

title: 1  Context
section: 1.2 How to Use this User Guide
text:
1.2 How to Use this User Guide
The ARC-GM created this guide to support individuals and teams who wish to apply the ARC‑GM RES approach in their own settings. It provides a step‑by‑step guide to planning, conducting, and communicating a Rapid Evidence Synthesis, from the initial formulation of questions through to interpretation and reporting.
The guide includes the following sections:

An introduction to Rapid Evidence Synthesis
Developing the RES protocol in collaboration with decision-makers
Searching for and selecting relevant research evidence
Extracting data and assessing the validity and certainty of findings
Synthesising and summarising the evidence
Interpreting and communicating findings

Given the above structure of this guide, you are recommended to start with its first section, and then work yourself through it one section after another. This is particularly true if you have no previous experience with any type of evidence synthesis.
Each section includes essential learning materials, further learning resources, practical tips, examples, and tools drawn from real‑world RES projects commissioned across the health and care system. We use text boxes to present examples, tips, and further learning resources.
After reading each section, you might find it helpful to complete the tasks listed at the end to consolidate your learning.
Throughout this guide, we embed helpful forms to facilitate the completion of your RES activities. Such forms are freely available and downloadable.
We also highlight jargon or terminologies using this colour. You can read their definitions or explanations by hovering your cursor over the highlighted text.
crumbs: 1  Context

title: 2  An Introduction to Rapid Evidence Synthesis
crumbs: 2  An Introduction to Rapid Evidence Synthesis

title: 4  Searching for and selecting relevant research evidence
text:
Understand how to develop a search strategy and how to search literature
Understand the streamlined approach to study selection
Understand the flexible approach to searching evidence for a RES
It is important to note that this section does not cover the know-how and hands-on skills and steps required to perform literature searches. Searching the literature effectively requires a certain level of skill and experience. Although beginners can carry out searches themselves, it is recommended to seek guidance and support from experienced librarians where possible. Those interested in learning systematic literature search methods are advised to refer to other resources, for example,
Chapter 4: Searching for and selecting studies, in the Cochrane Handbook for Systematic Reviews of Interventions.
Cochrane Library User Guide
PubMed® Online Training
Video Tutorials of searching the commonly used databases
crumbs: 4  Searching for and selecting relevant research evidence

title: 5  Extracting data and assessing the validity and certainty of findings
text:
Understand the streamlined approach to extracting information for a RES
Understand the streamlined approach to critically appraising research for a RES
Understand different approaches to critical appraisal for different types of research
Understand the GRADE-based approach to assessing the certainty of research evidence by considering study validity, relevance, consistency, and applicability.
crumbs: 5  Extracting data and assessing the validity and certainty of findings

title: 3  Developing the RES protocol in collaboration with decision-makers
text:
Understand the key components of a RES protocol (or plan)
Understand how to formulate decision-makers’ uncertainties into structured, answerable questions
Understand the flexible and iterative approach used when considering eligible research for a RES.

text: Understand how to write RES reports using clear and accessible language
